subject: Drop shell wrapper for docker compose operations
commit_message:
Drop shell wrapper for docker compose operations

Docker-compose configuration is already natively supported
within MassPnfSim module.

Change-Id: I26995126d8a26437821f0f8b254ff82752041b55
Issue-ID: INT-1623
Signed-off-by: Bartek Grzybowski <b.grzybowski@partner.samsung.com>

entries.message:

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2

would it make sense for G to create a dedicated repository integration/simulators/mass-pnf-simulator as we have dc-simulator or pnf-simulator? It could make the simu more visible, no?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2
> 
> would it make sense for G to create a dedicated repository integration/simulators/mass-pnf-simulator as we have dc-simulator or pnf-simulator? It could make the simu more visible, no?

In Integration repo there are several alike projects:

datafilecollector-testharness
emssimulator
hvvessimulator
mass-pnf-sim
netconf-pnp-simulator
pmsh-pnf-sim
pnf-onboarding
prov-mns-provider
sniroemulator

Do we want to move all of them to a separate repositories? If not then mass PNF simulator should be no exception in this regard i think. And if a decision on moving a particular subproject to a separate repo is to be made just on a factor of it's current support status then not sure either as now I'm contributing a lot but in few months it may stall again. 

So personally I'd leave it in Integration for now with a possibility to move it to a separate repo if some other factors emerge.

ok up to you
today we have 3 simulators in integration/simulators and the others in integration
I think it depends on the size, the complexity and the reusability
